opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My hands and arms broke out in these weird bullseye spots this morning, and now they are spreading to my chest. They feel hot and burning.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A sudden spreading bullseye-type rash on my hands/arms and now chest — I'm worried it's a serious reaction.

symptom_narrative.character: target/bullseye spots with concentric rings, mildly itchy and burning
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: backs of hands, forearms, spreading to the chest
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: mild malaise; no high fever or severe pain; no mouth/eye/genital sores
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: a cold sore on the lip ~10 days ago
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: independent; very anxious about the appearance

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a cold sore on the lip about 10 days ago

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no new medications, no recent antibiotics or anti-inflammatories

communication_style.typical_message_style: worried, focused on the dramatic appearance

objective_data_offered.value: symmetrical bullseye/target spots with three colour rings on the backs of the hands, forearms and chest; a healing cold-sore crust on the lower lip
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: camera view; mucosal surfaces assessed only by self-report

objective_data_offered.value: no sores or blisters in the mouth, eyes or genitals; no skin peeling
